standard_proposal:
Hi,

This looks like a strong match for my background.

Your project aligns well with the type of systems I build in Python: automation workflows, web scraping, structured data pipelines, and business-ready outputs.

Relevant experience:
- Built Streamlit dashboards that turn collected and processed data into usable business-facing tools with filters, metrics, and exports.
- Built Python scraping systems using BeautifulSoup and Playwright for both static and dynamic websites, with reliable parsing and structured output delivery.
- Created automated reporting and document generation workflows that produce structured PDF and export outputs from source data.

My approach would be to first confirm the source structure, required output, and any constraints, then build a reliable workflow that focuses on clean extraction, validation, and usable delivery.

I’m especially comfortable with dynamic sites when browser automation is needed, and I care a lot about making the final output clean, maintainable, and useful rather than delivering a fragile one-off script.

Best,
Artashes
premium_proposal:
Hi,

This is very well aligned with the kind of systems I build.

What stands out here is that this is not just a simple scraping task — it requires a reliable and scalable workflow.

Relevant experience:
- Built Streamlit dashboards that turn collected and processed data into usable business-facing tools with filters, metrics, and exports.
- Built Python scraping systems using BeautifulSoup and Playwright for both static and dynamic websites, with reliable parsing and structured output delivery.
- Created automated reporting and document generation workflows that produce structured PDF and export outputs from source data.

My core background is in Python automation, production-grade web scraping, Apify Actor development, and data pipelines. I focus on building systems that remain stable beyond the first run.

For this type of project, I would structure the solution so it can scale across multiple scrapers, not just solve a single case. That ensures long-term usability instead of a fragile one-off script.

I also consider practical challenges early, such as bot detection, dynamic content handling, and consistency of extracted data.

Best,
Artashes
